title: Multiply 2-digits by 1-digit with area models
description: Multiply a 1-digit number by a multi-digit number using area models.
topic_path: Multi-digit multiplication: place value and area models

assessment_items.question_content:
The following area model shows $6\times71$ broken into parts.   
*The image is not drawn to scale.*

[[☃ image 1]]

**Find the area of each part.**

$A=$ [[☃ input-number 1]] 


$B=$ [[☃ input-number 2]] 

**Find the total area.** 

$6\times71={}$ [[☃ input-number 5]]
hints:
The area of the whole rectangle is $6 \times 71$. 

[[☃ image 1]]

Now we add the areas to find the total.

[[☃ image 1]]

$\greenE{420}+\purpleD{6}=426$

So $6 \times 71 = 426$.

These are the areas:

$\begin{align}
&A=420\\\\
&B=6\\\\
&6 \times 71=426
\end{align}$

options.alt: An area model using a rectangle of 2 unequal parts, a first part labeled A with a width of 6 and a length of 70 and a second part to the right, labeled B, has the same width as part A and a length of 1.
